ex: Drei Mahlzeiten am Tag sind gesund.
n: Also an ironic exclamation: 'Mahlzeit!' = oh great, just great (sarcastic). Also a common midday greeting among colleagues.

ex: Ich fahre mit der U-Bahn zur Arbeit.
n: Short for Untergrundbahn. Munich has an excellent U-Bahn network.
'Was sind Sie von Beruf?' = What's your profession? 'berufstätig' = employed; 'der Berufsverkehr' = rush hour.
German CVs include a photo and date of birth — different from Anglo norms.
Parking in Munich is scarce and pricey. 'einen Parkplatz suchen' = to look for a space; 'das Parkhaus' = multi-storey car park.
'Frühstücken' = to have breakfast. German breakfast culture is elaborate.
Also: job offer or business proposal. 'Im Angebot' = on special offer/sale.
'Eine Frist einhalten' = to meet a deadline. 'Die Frist verpassen' = to miss a deadline.

ex: Die S-Bahn fährt bis zum Flughafen.
n: Short for Stadtschnellbahn. Connects city to suburbs and surrounding towns.

ex: Ich studiere an der Universität München.
n: Often abbreviated to 'Uni'. LMU and TUM are Munich's two main universities.
'bei Rot' = on red, 'bei Grün' = on green. Crossing on red as a pedestrian is frowned upon, even with no cars coming.
'Girokonto' = current account. 'Sparkonto' = savings account.
'die Heizung aufdrehen' = to turn the heating up. Heating costs (Heizkosten) are usually part of your Nebenkosten.
Getting a German Führerschein is notoriously expensive — often over €2000.

ex: Den Müll trennen wir hier streng.
n: Waste separation (Mülltrennung) is mandatory in Germany. Bins: Gelber Sack, Biotonne, Papiertonne, Restmüll.
'Eine Entscheidung treffen' = to make a decision.
'Ich habe Husten' = I have a cough. 'der Hustensaft' = cough syrup, bought at the Apotheke (pharmacy).
Usually used in plural: 'Überstunden machen'.
Often called 'Tram' colloquially. Munich has extensive tram (Tram) lines.
'sich bewerben' = to apply. Key word for job hunting in Germany.
Also: der Fahrstuhl. 'Außer Betrieb' = out of service — very common sign.
Also the grammatical present tense. Opposite: Vergangenheit (past), Zukunft (future).
'ins Krankenhaus kommen' = to go to hospital. Also: 'die Klinik'. The emergency entrance is die Notaufnahme.
Also: exchange rate (Kurs der Währung). Context clarifies.
'das digitale Zeitalter' = the digital age. More formal than 'die Zeit'; used for historical eras.
'Sich erkälten' = to catch a cold.
Munich Airport (Flughafen München, MUC) is one of Germany's largest. 'zum Flughafen fahren' = to go to the airport.
More formal than 'Kassenbon'. Needed for expense reports and returns.
'zu Mittag essen' = to have lunch. Traditionally the main hot meal of the day, often eaten around noon.
'im Büro' = at the office; 'ins Büro gehen' = to go to the office. 'das Großraumbüro' = open-plan office.

ex: Der Fortschritt in der KI ist beeindruckend.
n: 'Fortschritte machen' = to make progress.
The company that manages your building — your contact for repairs, Nebenkosten and the Hausordnung.
'Fieber messen' = to take a temperature. No plural in this sense.

ex: Ich habe eine Allergie gegen Katzen.
n: 'eine Allergie gegen ...' = an allergy to ... 'allergisch sein gegen' = to be allergic to. Pollen allergy = der Heuschnupfen.
'Krankenversicherung' = health insurance. Mandatory in Germany.
Usually used in plural: 'Hausaufgaben machen'.
Monthly white-collar pay (vs 'der Lohn' = wage). 'das Bruttogehalt' = gross; 'das Nettogehalt' = net, after tax.
Also means 'vacation trip'. Don't confuse with 'Feiertag' (public holiday).

ex: Die Vergangenheit kann man nicht ändern.
n: Also the grammatical past tense. Essential grammar vocabulary.

ex: Das Rezept meiner Oma ist geheim.
n: Two meanings: a cooking recipe and a medical prescription (der Arzt stellt ein Rezept aus). Context makes it clear.
'Einen Unterschied machen' = to make a difference. 'Kein Unterschied' = no difference.
Female doctor; male: der Arzt. 'zur Ärztin gehen' = to go to the doctor. Your GP is die Hausärztin.
Two meanings: price (cost) and prize/award. 'Preisverleihung' = award ceremony; 'Nobelpreis' = Nobel Prize.
Bavaria has more public holidays than most German states — 13 per year.

ex: Die Dauer des Kurses beträgt drei Monate.
n: 'Auf Dauer' = in the long run. No plural in standard usage.
Female colleague; male: der Kollege (a weak noun). 'eine nette Kollegin' = a nice colleague.
'Mehrwertsteuer' (MwSt) = VAT. 'Einkommensteuer' = income tax.

ex: Der Zug fährt in zehn Minuten.
n: 'mit dem Zug fahren' = to go by train. Run by Deutsche Bahn (DB); 'der Zug hat Verspätung' = the train is delayed.

ex: Gibt es einen Rabatt für Studenten?
n: '10% Rabatt' = 10% off. 'im Angebot' = on offer; 'der Schlussverkauf' = the end-of-season sales.
Female: die Mieterin.
'krank sein' = to be ill. 'die Krankenkasse' = your health insurer; 'die Krankschreibung' = a sick note.

ex: Auf der Autobahn ist ein Stau.
n: Germany is famous for its Autobahn but also for its legendary traffic jams around Munich.

ex: Nehmen Sie das Medikament dreimal täglich.
n: 'ein Medikament einnehmen' = to take a medication. Sold at the Apotheke; many require a Rezept (prescription).

ex: Ich lerne gerne in der Bibliothek.
n: Don't confuse with 'Buchhandlung' (bookshop). Free to use with a library card (Bibliotheksausweis).

ex: Die Apotheke ist gleich um die Ecke.
n: Marked by a red 'A' sign. Only place to buy medicine in Germany.
Female form: die Kollegin / die Kolleginnen.

ex: In München fahren viele Leute Fahrrad.
n: 'Radfahren' = to cycle. Munich has excellent bike infrastructure (Radwege).
German academic year: Wintersemester (Oct–Mar), Sommersemester (Apr–Sep).
'einen Vertrag unterschreiben' = to sign a contract. 'der Mietvertrag' (rental) and 'der Arbeitsvertrag' (employment) matter a lot in Germany.
'den Schlüssel verlieren' = to lose the key. Plural is identical: die Schlüssel. 'der Hausschlüssel' = house key.

ex: Die Bushaltestelle ist in der Nähe.
n: 'an der Bushaltestelle' = at the bus stop. In Munich the MVV runs the network; 'die Haltestelle' works for tram and bus too.
Also: der Gehweg, der Gehsteig (regional variations).
Also shortened to 'das Bad'. 'Die Dusche' = shower, 'die Badewanne' = bathtub.
Also: work reference letter (Arbeitszeugnis) — extremely important in German job applications.

ex: Ich bin bei der AOK versichert.
n: Germany's statutory health insurance system. Employees choose between funds (Kassen) like AOK, TK, or Barmer.

ex: Auf der Autobahn gibt es kein Tempolimit.
n: Many sections have no speed limit — but a recommended speed (Richtgeschwindigkeit) of 130 km/h applies.
Also means medical referral to a specialist. Germany is a Überweisungskultur.
Also means 'cuisine': 'die deutsche Küche' = German cuisine.
Germans traditionally eat a cold dinner (Abendbrot) — bread, cheese, cold cuts.
Germany's bottle deposit system. Return bottles at supermarket machines for a refund.

ex: An der Kasse zahle ich bar.
n: Queue at the Kasse. Have your bags ready — German cashiers are very fast!
'Eine Prüfung bestehen' = to pass. 'Durchfallen' = to fail.
The four are der Frühling, der Sommer, der Herbst, der Winter. Note: 'im Sommer' = in summer (im + season).

ex: Ich habe einen Termin beim Arzt.
n: Female: die Ärztin. 'Zum Arzt gehen' = to go to the doctor.

ex: Ich habe morgen einen Termin beim Zahnarzt.
n: Very broad usage: doctor, meeting, deadline. 'Termin machen/vereinbaren' = to make an appointment.
Can be by employer (dismissal) or employee (resignation). Context matters.

ex: Ich habe eine neue Stelle gefunden.
n: More specific than 'Beruf' — refers to a specific post or role.
Female: die Vermieterin. Opposite: der Mieter (tenant).

ex: Mein Chef ist oft im Ausland.
n: Female: die Chefin. In formal contexts: 'Vorgesetzter/Vorgesetzte'.

ex: Wir brauchen Benzin. Wo ist die nächste Tankstelle?
n: 'tanken' = to fill up. Handy in Germany — petrol stations are among the few shops open late and on Sundays.
Most Munich flats come with a Kellerabteil (storage cellar). 'im Keller' = in the basement.

ex: Er ist Student im zweiten Semester.
n: For school pupils: 'der Schüler'. Female university student: 'die Studentin'.
Major chains: REWE, Edeka, Lidl, Aldi, Penny. Sunday closures are the norm — plan ahead.
Finding one in Munich is famously hard. 'eine Wohnung mieten' = to rent a flat; '2-Zimmer-Wohnung' = a 2-room flat.
'im Wohnzimmer' = in the living room. Compound: Wohn- (living) + Zimmer (room).
'die Personalabteilung' = HR department. 'der Abteilungsleiter' = head of department.

ex: Die Miete in München ist sehr hoch.
n: 'Kaltmiete' = rent without utilities; 'Warmmiete' = rent including utilities.
Broad term. Includes Fachhochschule (university of applied sciences) — common in engineering.

ex: Ich habe viel Erfahrung in diesem Bereich.
n: 'Erfahrung sammeln' = to gain experience. 'aus Erfahrung' = from experience; 'die Berufserfahrung' = work experience.
'im Schlafzimmer' = in the bedroom. Compound: Schlaf- (sleep) + Zimmer (room).
'eine Sprache sprechen/lernen' = to speak/learn a language. 'die Muttersprache' = mother tongue; 'die Fremdsprache' = foreign language.
'Ich verstehe nur Bahnhof' = I don't understand a word (idiom).
Usually used in plural: 'Schmerzen haben'. Compounds: Kopfschmerzen, Bauchschmerzen, Rückenschmerzen.
Includes heating, water, building maintenance. Always used in plural.
Plastic/paper shopping bags cost money in German shops. Bring your own 'Stoffbeutel' (cloth bag).

ex: Der Unterricht beginnt um acht Uhr.
n: No plural in standard usage. 'Unterricht haben' = to have class/lessons.
'die Möglichkeit haben, etwas zu tun' = to have the chance to do something. 'nach Möglichkeit' = if possible.
Also the grammatical future tense. No plural in standard usage.

ex: Die Besprechung beginnt um neun Uhr.
n: More formal than 'Meeting' (also used in German offices).

ex: Die Wahl findet im September statt.
n: Also means 'choice': 'Du hast die Wahl' = It's your choice.
'eine Tasse Tee' = a cup of tea. 'der Kräutertee' = herbal tea. Ordering: 'Ich hätte gern einen Tee.'
'Angst haben vor' + Dative = to be afraid of. 'Keine Angst!' = Don't worry! / Don't be afraid!
'im Internet' = on the internet (im, not auf!). 'ins Internet gehen' = to go online; 'der Internetanschluss' = internet connection.
'eine Freundschaft schließen' = to make friends. Germans distinguish a 'Freund' (close friend) from a 'Bekannter' (acquaintance).
Germans tend to follow rules carefully. 'Vorschrift ist Vorschrift' = rules are rules.
'Am Ende' = in the end / at the end. 'Kein Ende in Sicht' = no end in sight.
'die Region München' = the Munich region. More colloquial alternative: 'die Gegend' (area).
'der Regen' is masculine. Munich gets significant rainfall. 'Saurer Regen' = acid rain.

ex: Ich habe die Hoffnung noch nicht aufgegeben.
n: 'Hoffnung aufgeben' = to give up hope. 'Die Hoffnung stirbt zuletzt' = hope dies last.
Borrowed from English. 'eine App herunterladen' = to download an app. The MVV app is essential for Munich transport.
Collective noun, rarely pluralised. Specific fruits: der Apfel, die Banane, etc.
Two senses: a computer program and a TV/event programme. 'das Fernsehprogramm' = the TV schedule.
Female: die Nachbarin. Important for apartment life in Germany.

ex: Er verbringt zu viel Zeit in sozialen Medien.
n: Always plural: 'die sozialen Medien' or 'Social Media'. Used in both forms.
Bavaria has strict beer purity laws (Reinheitsgebot, 1516). Masskrug = the 1-litre mug at Oktoberfest.
'das Olivenöl' = olive oil; 'das Sonnenblumenöl' = sunflower oil. Also fuel/heating oil (das Heizöl).
No plural in everyday use. 'mit/ohne Zucker' = with/without sugar — handy when ordering coffee. 'der Würfelzucker' = sugar cubes.
'Umweltschutz' = environmental protection. A central concept in German public life.

ex: Er ist heute in einer guten Stimmung.
n: Also: atmosphere/vibe of a place or event. 'Die Stimmung war ausgelassen' = the mood was exuberant.
Always used in plural: 'die Daten'. The singular 'das Datum' means a date (on a calendar).
'ein Gesetz' = a law; 'das Grundgesetz' = Germany's constitution. 'gegen das Gesetz' = against the law.

ex: Er interessiert sich nicht für Politik.
n: No plural in standard usage. 'Politiker/Politikerin' = politician.
'Sich Sorgen machen um' = to worry about. 'Keine Sorge!' = No worries!
'Handy' is a German false anglicism — English speakers say 'mobile phone' or 'cell phone'.

ex: In Bayern gibt es im Winter viel Schnee.
n: No plural. 'Es schneit' = it's snowing. Munich gets real snow in winter; 'der Schneeregen' = sleet.
Berlin is the Hauptstadt of Germany; Munich is the Landeshauptstadt of Bavaria. Compound: Haupt- (main) + Stadt (city).
'der Stress' is masculine. Borrowed from English. 'Gestresst sein' = to be stressed.
'Salz und Pfeffer' = salt and pepper. 'Könnten Sie mir das Salz reichen?' = Could you pass the salt?
'das Gewitter' is neuter. 'Der Sturm' = storm (general); 'das Gewitter' = thunderstorm specifically.
Often shared in the Keller of apartment buildings. Note the quiet hours and no laundry on Sundays. 'Wäsche waschen' = to do laundry.
'der Vorteil' is masculine. Opposite: der Nachteil (disadvantage). 'Vor- und Nachteile' = pros and cons.
Feminine in German. Also written 'Email'. Pronounced 'Ee-mail'.
'Es ist windig' = it's windy. 'der Gegenwind' = headwind. The plural (die Winde) is rare in everyday speech.
No plural in general usage. Types: Rindfleisch (beef), Schweinefleisch (pork), Hähnchenfleisch (chicken).

ex: Die Reise nach Italien war wunderschön.
n: 'Gute Reise!' = Have a good trip! 'Auf Reisen sein' = to be travelling.
Short form: die OP. 'sich operieren lassen' = to have an operation. Covered by the Krankenkasse if medically necessary.
Germany has over 300 types of bread. An important cultural institution!
'das Fenster' is neuter. 'Fenster öffnen/schließen/putzen' = to open/close/clean the window.
Also: quarter (a quarter of something). 'Viertelstunde' = 15 minutes; 'Viertel vor' = quarter to.
Das Gemüse is a singular collective noun, rarely pluralised. Specific veg: die Karotte, die Tomate, die Kartoffel.
'der Sonnenschein' is masculine. 'In der Sonne sitzen' = to sit in the sun.
'das Passwort' is neuter. 'Passwort zurücksetzen' = to reset password.

ex: Die Alpen sind nicht weit von München.
n: 'der Berg' is masculine. Munich is uniquely close to the Alps — a major draw.
The Isar is Munich's river — famous for the Eisbach surfing wave and riverside relaxation.
No plural. 'die gesunde Ernährung' = a healthy diet. 'sich ernähren' = to feed/nourish oneself.

ex: Ich war drei Monate im Ausland.
n: No plural. 'Im Ausland' = abroad (location). 'Ins Ausland' = abroad (direction/movement).

ex: Die Temperatur sinkt auf minus 10 Grad.
n: 'die Temperatur messen' = to take someone's temperature. Weather: 'Temperaturen um 20 Grad' = temperatures around 20°C.
'Fisch essen' = to eat fish. 'das Fischgeschäft' = the fishmonger's; Friday is a traditional fish day.

ex: Wir treffen uns in der Innenstadt.
n: 'die Innenstadt' is feminine. Synonym: 'das Stadtzentrum', 'die City'.
Also: meaning (of a word). 'Was ist die Bedeutung von...?' = What is the meaning of...?

ex: Ich möchte zwei Eier zum Frühstück.
n: Irregular plural: die Eier. 'das gekochte Ei' = boiled egg; 'das Spiegelei' = fried egg; 'das Rührei' = scrambled eggs.

ex: Bitte füllen Sie das Formular aus.
n: 'das Formular' is neuter. 'Formular ausfüllen' = to fill in a form. Essential for German bureaucracy.
'sich verändern' = to change (intransitive). 'etwas verändern' = to change something (transitive).
'Ehe schließen' = to get married (formal). Collocations: glückliche Ehe, Ehepartner (spouse).
Also: a pair of things (ein Paar Schuhe). Note: 'das' not 'die'.

ex: Das Möbel passt nicht durch die Tür.
n: 'Das Möbel' = one piece. 'Die Möbel' = furniture (almost always plural for multiple pieces).

ex: Ich habe eine Sprechstunde um 10 Uhr.
n: Literally 'speaking hour'. Doctor's consultation hours. 'Sprechstunde haben' = to hold consultation hours.

ex: Die Grenze zu Österreich ist nah.
n: Austria is very close to Munich — a popular day trip. The Schengen zone means open borders within the EU.
Also: connection or link between things.
'in den Kühlschrank stellen' = to put in the fridge. Compound: kühl (cool) + Schrank (cupboard).
Also: 'das Notebook'. Both masculine/neuter variants used.
Munich café culture is vibrant. 'Verlängerter' = lungo in Austria/Bavaria.
Also called die Spülmaschine. 'das Geschirr spülen' = to do the dishes. Plural is identical: die Geschirrspüler.
'die junge/ältere Generation' = the younger/older generation. 'der Generationenkonflikt' = the generation gap.
'Blutdruck messen' = to take blood pressure. High: hoher Blutdruck; low: niedriger Blutdruck.
Also: practice (putting something into practice). 'In der Praxis' = in practice.
'Der Strom' is masculine. Also: river current/stream. 'Stromausfall' = power cut.
Plural takes an umlaut: die Gäste. 'Gäste einladen' = to invite guests; 'der Gastgeber' = the host.
'Eine Lösung finden' = to find a solution. 'Problemlösung' = problem solving.
'die Tür öffnen/schließen' = to open/close the door. 'Tür zu!' = shut the door! 'die Haustür' = front door.
'das Gefühl' is neuter. 'Ein Gefühl haben für' = to have a feel for something.
'Aus welchem Grund?' = For what reason? 'Im Grunde genommen' = basically / fundamentally.

ex: Der einzige Nachteil ist der Preis.
n: Opposite of der Vorteil (advantage). 'Vor- und Nachteile' = pros and cons; 'im Nachteil sein' = to be at a disadvantage.

ex: Es ist eine Freude, hier zu sein.
n: 'Jemandem Freude machen' = to bring someone joy. 'Freude haben an' = to enjoy.

ex: Wir leben in einer freien Gesellschaft.
n: Also: company (legal entity: GmbH = Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung).
'Ein Problem lösen' = to solve a problem. 'Kein Problem!' is extremely common in Germany.
'Meiner Meinung nach' = in my opinion. 'Eine Meinung vertreten' = to hold an opinion.

ex: Der Klimawandel ist eine globale Herausforderung.
n: 'das Klima' is neuter. 'Klimawandel' = climate change — a huge topic in German discourse.

ex: Die Anmeldung muss schnell erledigt werden.
n: Legally required within 14 days of moving in Germany. Done at the Bürgeramt / Einwohnermeldeamt.
'Der Balkon' is masculine. Very desirable in Munich apartments.

ex: Mein Ziel ist es, fließend Deutsch zu sprechen.
n: Covers personal goals, business targets (KPIs) and destinations. 'Ziel erreichen' = to reach a goal; 'am Ziel ankommen' = to reach the destination.

ex: Heute sind viele Wolken am Himmel.
n: 'Es ist bewölkt' = it's cloudy. Don't confuse with 'die Cloud' (the English loanword) used for data storage.
Also: party (celebrating) — context clarifies. Major parties: CDU/CSU, SPD, Grüne, FDP, AfD.
'der Gedanke' is a weak masculine noun (des Gedankens). 'In Gedanken sein' = to be lost in thought.
Plural is rarely used. 'eine Scheibe Käse' = a slice of cheese. Colloquial: 'So ein Käse!' = What nonsense!

ex: Er ist ein Bekannter von mir.
n: Germans distinguish carefully between 'Bekannter' (acquaintance) and 'Freund' (close friend).
'das Netzwerk' is neuter. Also: professional network (berufliches Netzwerk).
'der Rotwein/Weißwein' = red/white wine; 'ein Glas Wein' = a glass of wine. Bavaria is beer country, but wine bars abound.
Borrowed from English. Also: 'die Webseite' (a single page) and 'die Homepage'. 'eine Website besuchen' = to visit a website.
For non-EU residents in Germany: always carry ID or passport. 'Personalausweis' = the national ID card.

ex: Wir wohnen im dritten Stock, kein Aufzug.
n: Also: a flight of stairs. 'Die Treppe hinaufgehen' = to go up the stairs.
'das Amt' is neuter. Key offices: Bürgeramt, Finanzamt, Ausländeramt. Germans interact with many Ämter.
'die Bevölkerung wächst' = the population is growing. Usually singular; 'die Einwohnerzahl' = the number of inhabitants.
'Wirtschaft' also means 'pub/inn' in some dialects! Context crucial.
Gender-neutral term for a person. 'Der Mensch' = human being (broader / more philosophical).

ex: Der Anfang ist immer am schwersten.
n: 'Am Anfang' = at the beginning. Opposite: das Ende (the end).
Includes hotels, Airbnbs, hostels, guesthouses (Pension). 'Unterkunft buchen' = to book accommodation.

ex: Das Hotel liegt direkt am See.
n: 'im Hotel übernachten' = to stay at a hotel; 'ein Zimmer buchen' = to book a room. Plural: die Hotels.
No plural. 'die Vollmilch' = whole milk; 'die Hafermilch' = oat milk. 'Kaffee mit Milch' = coffee with milk.
Borrowed from English; common in modern German workplaces. Also: 'die Frist' (the more formal term).
Every taxpayer deals with the Finanzamt. 'Steuererklärung' = tax return; deadlines are strict.
In Germany: Erdgeschoss = ground floor, 1. Stock = 1st floor (one above ground).
'Verantwortung übernehmen' = to take responsibility. 'Verantwortungsbewusstsein' = sense of responsibility.
A long compound word. Munich highlights: Marienplatz, Englischer Garten, Schloss Nymphenburg.

ex: Die Wäsche liegt noch im Keller.
n: 'Wäsche waschen' = to do laundry. 'Schmutzige Wäsche' = dirty laundry (literal & figurative).
'Ins Konzert gehen' = to go to a concert. Munich's Philharmoniker are world-renowned.

ex: Ich brauche ein Ticket für die Rückfahrt.
n: 'Hin- und Rückfahrt' = return trip. 'Einfache Fahrt' = one way. Key vocab for buying train tickets.
Forests are culturally significant in Germany. 'Waldsterben' (forest die-off) was a major 1980s concern.
'Doppelte Staatsbürgerschaft' = dual citizenship. Germany liberalised its naturalisation rules in 2024.
'Himmel' means both sky and heaven. 'Himmelblau' = sky blue. 'Um Himmels willen!
'eine Präsentation halten' = to give a presentation. Common at work; 'die Folie' = a slide.
'an einem Projekt arbeiten' = to work on a project. 'der Projektleiter' = project manager; 'die Frist' = the deadline.
'In der Realität' = in reality. Synonym: 'die Wirklichkeit'.
Also: homework exercise, duty, or role. Very versatile word.
Key document for non-EU residents in Germany. Obtained at the Ausländerbehörde.
Usually plural: 'Nachrichten' = news. 'Die Nachrichten schauen' = to watch the news.

ex: Ich möchte auf eine Insel reisen.
n: 'auf einer Insel' = on an island (auf, not in!). German islands include Sylt, Rügen and Norderney.
Each apartment has a labelled letterbox (Briefkasten) in the building entrance.
No plural (collective noun). Emergency number: 110. 'Polizist/in' = police officer.
'das Wissen' is neuter. 'Kenntnisse' = specific knowledge/skills. 'Allgemeinwissen' = general knowledge.
'Gute Fahrt!' = Have a good journey! 'Hinfahrt' = outward journey; 'Rückfahrt' = return journey.
Abbreviated 'Str.' in addresses (e.g. Leopoldstr.). 'auf der Straße' = in the street; 'die Einbahnstraße' = one-way street.

ex: Wir treffen uns auf dem Marienplatz.
n: 'der Platz' is masculine. Also: space/seat. 'Ist hier noch Platz?' = Is there still room here?
Munich's art scene: the Pinakotheken (world-class museums) and many galleries. 'Kunstwerk' = work of art.
Germany's social safety net: Arbeitslosengeld (unemployment), Kindergeld (child benefit), Wohngeld (housing benefit).
Very popular in Munich. The Viktualienmarkt is the famous central market — fresh produce, flowers, cheese.
Also: 'Bluttest'. Usually done at the doctor's or at a lab (Labor).
Two unrelated meanings: a dish/meal (Hauptgericht = main course) and a court of law ('vor Gericht' = in court). Context decides.

ex: Die Kühe stehen auf dem Feld.
n: 'das Feld' is neuter. Also: field of expertise. 'Das Feld der Wissenschaft' = the field of science.

ex: Es gibt eine Ausnahme von der Regel.
n: 'In der Regel' = as a rule / normally. 'Die Ausnahme bestätigt die Regel' = the exception proves the rule.
Used exclusively for fellow university students. Female: die Kommilitonin.
Where non-EU residents register, extend visas, and apply for permanent residence. Essential for visa matters.
'der See' (masc.) = lake; 'die See' (fem.) = the sea! Both exist — a very common confusion.
Munich has many bridges over the Isar. 'Brücken schlagen' = to build bridges (metaphorically).
Not relevant for landlocked Munich, but essential vocab for port cities like Hamburg and Bremen.
Informal: 'Oma'. Male: der Großvater / Opa.

ex: Was für eine Art Problem ist das?
n: 'Eine Art von' = a kind of. 'Auf diese Art und Weise' = in this way. 'Eigenart' = a peculiarity.

ex: Die Feuerwehr ist sehr schnell da.
n: Emergency number: 112 (also for ambulance). The Feuerwehr is highly respected in Germany.
'Redefreiheit' = freedom of speech; 'Pressefreiheit' = press freedom — core constitutional values.
'Forscher/in' = researcher; 'Forschungsergebnisse' = research results. Big in Munich (Max Planck, TUM).
'sich impfen lassen' = to get vaccinated. Impfpass = vaccination record.
'Wissenschaftler/in' = scientist. 'Geisteswissenschaften' = humanities; 'Naturwissenschaften' = natural sciences.
Informal: 'Opa'. Female: die Großmutter / Oma.

ex: Ich bin der Ansicht, dass das falsch ist.
n: More formal than 'Meinung'. 'Der Ansicht sein, dass...' = to be of the view that...
'der Vollmond' = full moon; 'auf dem Mond' = on the moon. The plural (Monde) refers to moons of other planets.

ex: Das Grundgesetz ist das höchste Gesetz.
n: Germany's constitution (1949). 'Grundgesetz' literally = basic/fundamental law.
Borrowed from English; widely used in German offices. 'Rückmeldung' is the German equivalent.
'Ratenzahlung' = instalment plan. 'Monatliche Rate' = monthly instalment. Common for big purchases.
Plural 'Kinder' is one of the most common words in German. 'Kindheit' = childhood.
'das Familienmitglied' is neuter. Compound: Familie + Mitglied.
'Lohn' = hourly/weekly wage; 'Gehalt' = monthly salary. 'Mindestlohn' = minimum wage.
'Jemanden befördern' = to promote someone. Also: transportation (moving something/someone).
'der Traum' is masculine. 'Traumjob' = dream job. 'Träumen' = to dream.
Germans tip by rounding up. Say 'Stimmt so' (keep the change) or state the total you want to pay.
Also: 'die Strecke' (stretch/distance). Use 'Route' for navigation, 'Strecke' for the physical road.
'Ins Kino gehen' = to go to the cinema. 'OmU' = Original mit Untertiteln (original with subtitles).
'Gewicht verlieren' = to lose weight. 'Zunehmen' = to gain weight; 'Abnehmen' = to lose weight.
Often used in plural: 'Informationen'. 'Auskunft' = information (at a desk or by phone).

ex: Ich lese gerade ein spannendes Buch.
n: 'Bücher leihen' = to borrow books (from the Bibliothek). 'Bücher kaufen' = to buy books.
Borrowed from English. 'Kleinigkeit essen' = more German way of saying snack.
Adjective used as noun. Female: die Erwachsene. Plural: die Erwachsenen.
Very versatile: transport connection, phone connection, chemical bond or personal link — all 'Verbindung'.
Borrowed from English; plural die Babys. 'ein Baby bekommen' = to have a baby. 'das Kleinkind' = toddler.
Opposite of der Ausgang (exit). 'der Haupteingang' = the main entrance; 'Eingang' often marks a door you enter by.

ex: Der Erfolg kommt nicht von alleine.
n: 'der Erfolg' is masculine. 'Erfolgreich' = successful. 'Viel Erfolg!' = Good luck / best of success!

ex: Können Sie mir eine Rechnung ausstellen?
n: 'Rechnung' = invoice/bill. 'Die Rechnung stimmt nicht' = the bill is wrong. Also: an arithmetic calculation.
More formal than 'Firma'. 'Unternehmer' = entrepreneur. Often interchangeable.
'Die Sonne scheint' = the sun is shining. 'der Sonnenschein' = sunshine; 'die Sonnenbrille' = sunglasses.
'Rückenschmerzen' = back pain (a very common complaint). 'Den Rücken stärken' = to strengthen the back.
'die Stadt' is feminine. 'Innenstadt' = city centre; 'Großstadt' = major city; 'Altstadt' = old town.

ex: Vertrauen ist die Basis jeder Beziehung.
n: 'das Vertrauen' is neuter. 'Jemandem vertrauen' = to trust someone. 'Vertrauen aufbauen' = to build trust.

ex: Neugier ist der Motor des Lernens.
n: Also: 'die Neugierde'. 'Aus Neugier' = out of curiosity.

ex: Zum Nachtisch hätte ich gerne Eis.
n: 'Nachtisch' literally = 'after table'. Also: 'das Dessert' (borrowed from French).
Common Beilagen: Kartoffeln, Reis, Salat, Gemüse. 'Mit welcher Beilage?' = Which side would you like?
Less relevant within the Eurozone but useful when travelling. Banks also offer Geldwechsel (currency exchange).
'Backofen' = oven; 'Herd' = stovetop/cooker. 'Den Ofen vorheizen' = to preheat the oven.

ex: Die Erde dreht sich um die Sonne.
n: Also: soil/ground (die Erde im Garten). 'Auf der Erde' = on Earth. 'Erdgeschoss' = ground floor.
Major German papers: Süddeutsche Zeitung (Munich-based), FAZ, Die Zeit, Der Spiegel.
'der Fehler' = mistake/error/defect. 'Einen Fehler machen' = to make a mistake.
'Eine Scheibe' = a slice; also a disc or pane of glass. 'scheibenweise' = slice by slice.
Also: an evening out. 'Ausgang haben' (military) = to have leave. 'Notausgang' = emergency exit.
Adjective used as noun. Female: die Jugendliche. Plural: Jugendliche.
'Made in Germany' is globally associated with Qualität. 'Qualitätsprodukt' = quality product.
Munich has outstanding museums: Deutsches Museum, the three Pinakotheken, Haus der Kunst, BMW Museum.
'Die Wahrheit sagen' = to tell the truth. Opposite: die Lüge (lie).

ex: In der Fußgängerzone darf man nicht fahren.
n: Munich's Marienplatz area is a large Fußgängerzone. Very common in German city centres.
Germany has separate bins: Biotonne (organic), Papiertonne (paper), Restmüll (general waste).

ex: Ich arbeite für eine kleine Firma.
n: Slightly more colloquial than 'Unternehmen': 'Firma' in speech, 'Unternehmen' in formal writing.

ex: Wir gehen heute Abend ins Theater.
n: Munich has world-class opera and theatre. The Nationaltheater hosts the Bayerische Staatsoper.
'die Karte' is feminine. Also: playing card, ticket, menu, map. 'Kreditkarte' = credit card.
Male form. Female: die Mitarbeiterin. Neutral plural: Mitarbeitende (modern, gender-inclusive usage).

ex: Der Zug fährt auf Gleis vier ab.
n: 'das Gleis' = track/platform number. At the station: 'Auf welchem Gleis?' = On which platform?
Note: the 'k' is pronounced in German. Singular and plural are spelled the same: die Knie. 'Mir tut das Knie weh' = my knee hurts.

ex: Die Küste der Nordsee ist flach.
n: 'an der Küste' = on the coast. Germany's coasts are die Nordsee and die Ostsee — far from landlocked Munich.
'das Geschwister' (one sibling) is rare. Usually plural: 'die Geschwister' = siblings.

ex: Ich habe eine gute Erinnerung daran.
n: 'Sich erinnern an' = to remember. 'Zur Erinnerung' = as a reminder. 'In Erinnerung bleiben' = to be remembered.

ex: Biegen Sie an der Kreuzung links ab.
n: 'an der Kreuzung' = at the intersection. 'rechts/links abbiegen' = to turn right/left. From 'kreuzen' = to cross.

ex: Die Nordsee ist rau im Winter.
n: 'die See' (fem.) = sea. 'der See' (masc.) = lake. Critical distinction!
'Eine Idee haben' = to have an idea. 'Keine Idee' = no idea. Ultimately from Greek via French.
Munich's Pinakotheken house world-famous collections. 'Bild' is the broader term for picture/image.
Pedestrians have right of way. Jaywalking (crossing on red) is frowned upon — even with no cars around.
Adjective used as noun. Also: foreigner/outsider. 'In der Fremde' = in a foreign land / far from home.
'Voraussetzungen erfüllen' = to meet the requirements. Key word in job ads.
A hot topic in German politics. 'Integrationskurs' = integration course including language and culture classes.
Munich's Englischer Garten is one of the world's largest urban parks — bigger than New York's Central Park.

ex: Wir haben viel Zeug auf dem Dachboden.
n: The attic/loft of a building. Plural takes an umlaut: die Dachböden. Often used for storage, like der Keller.
'Kulturschock' = culture shock; 'Hochkultur' = high culture. Munich is a major cultural hub.
Anglicism, widely used: 'Das System braucht ein Update.' 'Bitte updaten' = please update.
First point of contact in the German healthcare system. Issues referrals (Überweisungen) and sick notes.

ex: Ich habe einen Gutschein für das Restaurant.
n: Common birthday/Christmas gift in Germany. 'Gutschein einlösen' = to redeem a voucher.
Volunteering (Ehrenamt) is highly valued in Germany; many social services rely on volunteers (Ehrenamtliche).

ex: Die Mitgliedschaft kostet 20 Euro im Monat.
n: 'Mitglied werden' = to become a member. German clubs (Vereine) have millions of members nationwide.
Part of das Besteck (cutlery): die Gabel, das Messer, der Löffel. 'mit Messer und Gabel essen' = to eat with a knife and fork.

ex: Die Überschwemmung hat großen Schaden angerichtet.
n: Munich and Bavaria are periodically affected by flooding (Hochwasser) of the Isar and other rivers.
Also said when someone sneezes: 'Gesundheit!' = Bless you! One of the most useful everyday words.

ex: Er geht nächstes Jahr in Rente.
n: 'In Rente gehen' = to retire. Germany's pension age is gradually increasing to 67.

ex: Am Autobahnkreuz geht es nach links.
n: Where two Autobahnen meet — Munich is ringed by several. 'das Autobahndreieck' = a three-way junction.
Also: 'das Display' (borrowed). 'Bildschirmzeit' = screen time. Compound: Bild (image) + Schirm (screen).
'Jemanden überraschen' = to surprise someone. 'Überraschungsparty' = surprise party.
'die Automobilindustrie' = the car industry, huge around Munich (BMW). 'die Industrie' = the sector as a whole.

ex: Ich habe ein Monatsabo für die MVV.
n: Short for Abonnement. 'Monatsabo' / 'Jahresabo'. The MVV runs Munich's public transport network.

ex: Wir machen an der Raststätte Pause.
n: A motorway services with food, fuel and toilets. 'eine Pause an der Raststätte machen' = to take a break at the services.
At level crossings (Bahnübergang). 'Grenzen/Schranken setzen' = to set limits (metaphorical).
Many Munich apartment buildings have an Innenhof — a shared courtyard. Quiet-hours rules apply.
Female: die Kundin. 'Kundendienst' = customer service. 'Stammkunde' = regular customer.

ex: Es braucht Mut, das zu sagen.
n: 'Mut haben' = to be brave. 'Jemandem Mut machen' = to encourage someone. 'Mutlos' = discouraged.
Anglicism widely used in German. 'Im Homeoffice arbeiten' = to work from home.

ex: Beim Zoll muss man die Waren angeben.
n: Also: inch (unit of measurement). 'Beim Zoll' = at customs. Within Schengen, there are no customs checks.
German doctors' waiting rooms can mean long waits. Having an appointment (Termin) helps, but isn't a guarantee.
Adjective used as noun. Female: die Angestellte. Distinguished from 'Arbeiter' (manual worker).
University lectures. 'Seminar' = a smaller, discussion-based class. Both are typical parts of a degree.
'das Handy laden' = to charge the phone. Compound: laden (to charge) + Kabel (cable). Plural identical: die Ladekabel.

ex: Die Luft hier ist sehr frisch.
n: 'An die frische Luft gehen' = to go out for fresh air. 'Luftverschmutzung' = air pollution.
'Ohne Zweifel' = without doubt. 'Im Zweifel' = in doubt. 'Zweifeln an' = to doubt (something).
No plural; feminine (die Butter). 'ein Brot mit Butter' = bread and butter; 'die Erdnussbutter' = peanut butter.
Popular Munich tours: hop-on-hop-off bus, Marienplatz walking tours, and Dachau memorial trips.

ex: Wir wohnen in einem ruhigen Vorort.
n: Munich's central districts (Schwabing, Neuhausen, Maxvorstadt) are popular; true Vororte lie further out.
Also: travel guidebook. 'Lonely Planet' and 'Reise Know-How' are popular series. Context clarifies which.

ex: Die Landschaft in Bayern ist wunderschön.
n: Bavaria's landscape includes the Alps, lakes, and rolling countryside — a big draw for residents.
'Haushalt führen' = to run a household. 'Staatshaushalt' = state budget. Compound: Haus + halten.
Watch the signs when driving or cycling in Munich. Figuratively, 'eine Einbahnstraße' can mean a dead-end situation.
Where you do your Anmeldung and get ID cards and certificates. Book an appointment online well in advance.
'Bachelorarbeit' / 'Masterarbeit' / 'Doktorarbeit' (doctoral thesis). A key academic milestone.

ex: Ich habe Angst vor dem Zahnarzt.
n: Female: die Zahnärztin. 'Zum Zahnarzt gehen' = to go to the dentist. Insurance covers basic dental care.
Usually plural: 'die Zinsen'. 'Zinssatz' = interest rate. 'Habenzinsen' = credit interest.
'Antwort geben' = to give an answer. 'Um Antwort wird gebeten' (u.A.w.g.) = RSVP / please reply.
'Thema wechseln' = to change the subject. 'Kein Thema!' = No problem! (colloquial).
'Ein Risiko eingehen' = to take a risk. 'Risikofreudig' = risk-taking; 'risikoavers' = risk-averse.
'Bachelor-/Master-Abschluss', 'Doktortitel'. 'Den Abschluss machen' = to graduate.
'Einwanderer' = immigrant; 'Auswanderer' = emigrant. Germany receives large numbers of immigrants.
'Umtauschen' = to exchange; 'Rückgabe' = return. Both usually require a receipt in German shops.

ex: Ich bin seit drei Jahren Selbstständiger.
n: 'Selbstständig sein' = to be self-employed. 'Freiberufler' = freelancer — a related but distinct tax category.

ex: Ich habe eine gute Note bekommen.
n: German grades: 1 (sehr gut) = best, 6 (ungenügend) = worst — the opposite of many Anglo systems.
Cycling on the pavement (Bürgersteig) is illegal in Germany. Always use the Radweg (cycle path).
'Geduld haben' = to have patience. 'Jemandes Geduld auf die Probe stellen' = to test someone's patience.

ex: Bitte achten Sie auf die Aufmerksamkeit.
n: 'Aufmerksamkeit schenken' = to pay attention. 'aufmerksam sein' = to be attentive/alert.
'Nachtfrost' = overnight frost. Common in Munich in late autumn/winter.
'Asylbewerber' = asylum seeker. A major topic post-2015. 'Geflüchtete' is now the preferred neutral term.

ex: Ich kaufe ein Souvenir für meine Familie.
n: Munich souvenirs: Dirndl, Lederhosen, Masskrug, Weißwurst sets — and of course Oktoberfest merchandise.
'Eine Frage stellen' = to ask a question. 'Außer Frage stehen' = to be beyond question.

ex: Ich hätte gerne eine Tasse Kaffee.
n: 'eine Tasse Kaffee' = a cup of coffee. Compare die Tasse (cup) with das Glas (glass) and der Becher (mug).

ex: Die Vorlesung findet im Hörsaal statt.
n: A university lecture hall; plural die Hörsäle. From hören (to listen) + Saal (hall). 'die Vorlesung' is held there.
Also: 'Arbeitsunfähigkeitsbescheinigung' (AU) — the official sick note from the doctor for your employer.

ex: Wir essen gerne Eis im Sommer.
n: Means both 'ice' (Glatteis = black ice, Eiswürfel = ice cube) and 'ice cream' (Eisdiele = parlour). Context clarifies.
'Sport treiben/machen' = to do sport. Football (Fußball) is most popular; FC Bayern is Munich's club.

ex: Er ist ein Experte auf diesem Gebiet.
n: Also: 'der Fachmann/Fachfrau'. 'Expertin' = female expert.

ex: Abends sitzen wir auf der Terrasse.
n: Very desirable feature in Munich apartments. Ground-floor equivalent of Balkon.
'Im Zusammenhang mit' = in connection with. 'Aus dem Zusammenhang reißen' = to take out of context.
'Handgepäck' = hand luggage; 'aufgegebenes Gepäck' = checked luggage. No plural (collective noun).
'Flaschenpfand' = bottle deposit. Return at supermarket for refund.

ex: Mein Freundeskreis ist klein aber fein.
n: Germans typically keep smaller, closer friend groups (Freundeskreise) than in many other cultures.
'Neid haben auf' = to envy. 'Neidisch sein' = to be envious/jealous.
Plural is identical: die Teller. 'der Suppenteller' = soup plate; 'der Teller ist leer' = the plate is empty.
No plural in standard usage. 'Musik machen' = to make music. Munich has strong classical and club scenes.
'das Glas' is neuter. 'Ein Glas Wein' = a glass of wine. Also: the material glass.

ex: Die Daten sind in der Cloud gespeichert.
n: Borrowed from English. Rarely: 'die Datenwolke'. 'In der Cloud' = in the cloud.
A price list or rate card. 'auf der Preisliste' = on the price list. Compound: Preis (price) + Liste (list).
German portions are often generous. 'Halbportion' = half portion (often available for children).
Gender-neutral in modern usage. 'Lebenspartner' = life partner. Female: die Partnerin.
'Einen Kredit beantragen' = to apply for a loan. Germans are traditionally conservative about debt.
A growing concern in modern Germany. 'Einsam sein' = to be lonely.

ex: Wir nehmen ein Taxi zum Flughafen.
n: Also available: Uber, FreeNow. Munich taxis are yellow and regulated.

ex: Das Ministerium gibt eine Stellungnahme ab.
n: Bavaria has its own state ministries (Bayerische Staatsministerien) and broad regional autonomy.

ex: Sie macht eine Ausbildung zur Köchin.
n: Germany's dual Ausbildung system is world-famous: it combines paid work with vocational-school training.

ex: Das Gerät hat zwei Jahre Garantie.
n: 'Garantie geben' = to give a guarantee. 'Gewährleistung' = the statutory warranty (two years, legally required).
Bavaria is known for strong traditions: Oktoberfest, Tracht (traditional dress), and the Maibaum (maypole).

ex: Im Herbst gibt es oft Nebel in München.
n: Autumn fog is very common in Munich and the surrounding Alpine foothills.
Part of das Besteck; plural identical: die Messer. 'das Küchenmesser' = kitchen knife; 'scharf' = sharp.

ex: Was sind die Folgen dieser Entscheidung?
n: Also: episode (of a TV series), e.g. 'Folge 3' = Episode 3. 'Infolgedessen' = as a result.
Masculine in standard German; some speakers say 'das Joghurt'. Both are acceptable.
'Die Ausnahme bestätigt die Regel' = the exception proves the rule. 'In Ausnahmefällen' = in exceptional cases.
Plural is identical: die Löffel. Recipes use 'der Teelöffel' (tsp) and 'der Esslöffel' (tbsp); 'der Suppenlöffel' = soup spoon.
'eine Investition machen' = to make an investment; 'in etwas investieren' = to invest in something. 'die Geldanlage' = a financial investment.
'Zum Beispiel' (z.B.) = for example. One of the most common phrases in academic and everyday German.

ex: Der Hausarzt überweist mich zum Facharzt.
n: In Germany you usually need a referral (Überweisung) from your GP (Hausarzt) to see a Facharzt (specialist).
Collective noun for all employees of a company. No gender distinction.

ex: Der Zug hat zehn Minuten Verspätung.
n: 'Verspätung haben' = to be delayed. Very common on Deutsche Bahn. The opposite of 'pünktlich' (punctual).
Short for 'Akkumulator'. 'Den Akku laden' = to charge the battery. 'Der Akku ist leer' = the battery is dead.

ex: Beim Metzger kaufe ich frisches Fleisch.
n: Also refers to the butcher (person). 'Metzgerei' = butcher's shop; Bavarian Metzgereien are excellent.

ex: Die Bäckerei öffnet um sechs Uhr.
n: German bakeries are legendary: fresh bread, Brötchen (rolls), and pretzels (Brezeln).
A core value of the German Basic Law. 'Wehrhafte Demokratie' = a democracy that defends itself against extremism.
Also: criticism (a critique). 'Konstruktive Kritik' = constructive criticism. Also a review (Filmkritik).
Documents apartment condition at move-in/out. Critical for getting Kaution back.
Go to the Notaufnahme only for genuine emergencies; for non-urgent issues, call the on-call service (116117).
DAAD, Deutschlandstipendium, Bayerische Begabtenförderung — many scholarship options exist for students.
Key word in ADAS: LiDAR-, Kamera-, Radar-Sensor. 'Sensorik' = sensor systems / sensing.
Usually plural: 'die Menschenrechte'. Enshrined in Germany's Basic Law (Grundgesetz), Articles 1–19.
'Methodisch vorgehen' = to proceed methodically. 'Methodik' = methodology.
'Prioritäten setzen' = to set priorities. 'Vorrang haben' = to take precedence.
'In Kraft treten' = to come into effect (law). 'Nebenwirkung' = side effect. 'Ursache und Wirkung' = cause and effect.
German health insurance covers many Vorsorgeuntersuchungen — cancer screenings, dental checks, and more.
Powerful in Germany: IG Metall (automotive), Ver.di (services), IG BCE (chemical/energy sector).
Germans love dogs. 'Hundesteuer' = dog tax (yes, it exists). 'Hunde verboten' = no dogs allowed.
Oktoberfest = the world's most famous Fest. Also: Weihnachtsfest (Christmas) and countless Stadtfeste.
BMW, Siemens, MAN, Allianz, and Munich Re are all headquartered in Munich — major employers.
'Freiberufler' (m) / 'Freiberuflerin' (f). Freelancers have different tax rules from Gewerbetreibende (traders).
'Verschiedene Aspekte berücksichtigen' = to consider various aspects. Common in academic writing.
Bringing Blumen as a host gift is common in Germany — always an odd number of flowers (except a dozen).
Short for Wohngemeinschaft. Very common for students and young professionals, especially in pricey Munich.

ex: Sie hat eine Veröffentlichung in Nature.
n: Also: 'das Paper' (research paper, Anglicism). 'Publizieren' = to publish. Key for researchers.

ex: Ich habe Ersparnisse auf der Bank.
n: Usually plural. 'Sparen' = to save money. 'Sparsamkeit' = frugality — a valued German virtue.
Key vocab for doctor's visits. 'Symptome beschreiben' = to describe symptoms.
Notoriously hard and expensive in Germany. Theory + practical exam; the whole process averages over €2000.
'Kulturelle Vielfalt' = cultural diversity; 'Artenvielfalt' = biodiversity. 'vielfältig' = diverse/varied.
Read every clause carefully. German tenancy law (Mietrecht) strongly protects tenants.
In cities: 50 km/h; residential zones: 30 km/h; Autobahn: no general limit, but a recommended 130 km/h.
Germany has 16 Bundesländer. Bavaria (Bayern) is the largest by area; Munich is its capital.

ex: Ich höre täglich Podcasts auf Deutsch.
n: A great way to learn German! Popular: 'Slow German', Deutschlandfunk, 'Fest & Flauschig'.
'Die Bäume' = the trees. 'Baumscheibe' = tree pit. Street trees (Strassenbäume)
'Eine Herausforderung annehmen' = to accept a challenge. Very common in German professional contexts.

ex: Bildung ist der Schlüssel zum Erfolg.
n: Encompasses both formal education and cultural refinement. 'Bildungsweg' = educational path.
Many Munich museums offer free entry on Sundays. 'Eintritt frei' = free admission.
Also: software development (Softwareentwicklung), child development. 'Forschung und Entwicklung' = R&D.

ex: Als Ausländer muss ich mich anmelden.
n: Female: die Ausländerin. Not pejorative in itself. 'Expat' is increasingly used in international cities.
Required for German citizenship. Covers German history, law, and democratic values (33 questions).
'Renovieren' = to renovate. 'Frisch renoviert' = freshly renovated — a key selling point in flat listings.
Often required for rental and visa applications, and other official forms in Germany.

ex: Eine Brezel und ein Bier, bitte!
n: Iconic Bavarian snack, also spelled 'Breze' in Bavaria. Available at every bakery.
Borrowed from English. 'Im Trend liegen' = to be trendy. 'Gegentrend' = counter-trend.
Borrowed from French. Also: 'die Brieftasche' or 'die Geldbörse' (more common in Austria/Switzerland).
Germany's standard debit card (Girocard). More widely accepted than credit cards in Germany.

ex: Wir machen eine Führung durch das Schloss.
n: Also: management/leadership. 'Unter Führung von' = under the leadership of.

ex: Wir blicken optimistisch in die Zukunft.
n: Also: perspective/viewpoint. 'Berufliche Perspektiven' = career prospects; 'keine Perspektive' = no prospects.
'Haustier' = pet; 'Wildtier' = wild animal. 'Tierarzt' = vet; 'Tierschutz' = animal protection.
Standard in Germany: 1–3 months' cold rent. Must be returned within a reasonable time after move-out.
Core concept in ML/DL. 'Lernalgorithmus' = learning algorithm. Plural: Algorithmen.
'Strategisch denken' = to think strategically. 'Geschäftsstrategie' = business strategy.
'Motiviert sein' = to be motivated; 'demotiviert' = demotivated. A key word in work contexts.

ex: Mein Mentor hat mir viel beigebracht.
n: Female: die Mentorin. Mentoring programmes (Mentorenprogramme) are common at German universities.
'Streiken' = to strike. German unions exercise their Streikrecht (right to strike) regularly.
'Gut zusammenarbeiten' = to collaborate well. 'Teamarbeit' = teamwork — a core workplace value.
Also: legal trial/case. 'Prozessoptimierung' = process optimisation. 'im Prozess' = in progress.
Legally required on request. Germans decode coded phrasing: 'stets zu unserer Zufriedenheit' actually means 'merely adequate'.
A legally mandated employee-representation body in German companies with five or more employees.
Female: die Touristin. Munich gets ~7 million tourists a year; Oktoberfest alone draws millions.

ex: Ich kaufe eine Wochenkarte für die MVV.
n: MVV = Munich's transport authority. Ticket types: Wochenkarte (weekly), Monatskarte (monthly), Jahreskarte (annual).
'Anforderungen erfüllen' = to meet requirements. A key word in job descriptions (Stellenanzeigen).
Borrowed from French. 'Auf hohem Niveau' = at a high level. 'niveaulos' = lacking class/standards.

ex: Ich mache ein Praktikum bei Siemens.
n: Pflichtpraktikum = mandatory; freiwilliges Praktikum = optional. Common before or during studies. Plural: Praktika.

ex: Der Garten ist im Frühling schön.
n: 'Der Biergarten' (beer garden) is a Bavarian institution; the Englischer Garten is Munich's huge city park.
'Bio' prefix = organic; 'Biomarkt' = organic supermarket. Germany is one of Europe's largest organic markets.
'Ein Konzept erstellen' = to create a concept. 'Konzeptpapier' = concept paper.
'Frustriert sein' = to be frustrated. 'Einen Frust haben' is colloquial for being fed up.

ex: Er ist eine Autorität auf seinem Gebiet.
n: Also: authority (power/control). 'Elterliche Autorität' = parental authority. Also a respected expert.
Psychotherapie = psychotherapy; Physiotherapie = physiotherapy — both widely available in Germany.

ex: Die Lebenshaltungskosten in München sind hoch.
n: Always plural. Munich is one of Germany's most expensive cities — plan your budget carefully.

ex: Das Modell wurde mit Daten trainiert.
n: Context-dependent: ML model, fashion model, architectural model, business model.

ex: Die Miete wird per Lastschrift abgebucht.
n: 'SEPA-Lastschrift' = the European direct-debit standard. Common for recurring payments like rent and bills.
'In der Öffentlichkeit' = in public. Related: 'öffentliche Verkehrsmittel' = public transport.
Abbreviated KI (German) = AI (English). 'KI-Modell', 'KI-System'. Central to ADAS and ML work.

ex: Samstags gehe ich auf den Markt.
n: Munich's Viktualienmarkt is world-famous. Also: the Wochenmarkt found in every neighbourhood.

ex: Das ist die Grundlage unseres Projekts.
n: 'Auf der Grundlage von' = on the basis of. 'Grundlagen schaffen' = to lay foundations / create a basis.
Also spelled 'Potential'. 'Entwicklungspotenzial' = growth potential. Common in business and tech.

ex: Ich arbeite heute in der Nachtschicht.
n: Frühschicht = early shift, Spätschicht = late shift, Nachtschicht = night shift.
'Möbliert' = furnished; 'teilmöbliert' = partly furnished; 'unmöbliert' = unfurnished.

ex: Er nimmt an einer Konferenz teil.
n: Also: 'die Tagung' (academic conference). CVPR, NeurIPS etc. are major international Konferenzen.

ex: Wegen der Baustelle gibt es Stau.
n: Munich is famous for perpetual Baustellen. Colloquially, 'Das ist eine Baustelle' = it's still a work in progress.
'Stolz sein auf' = to be proud of. 'Nationalstolz' = national pride; 'Berufsstolz' = professional pride.
The standard rental notice period in Germany is 3 months — check your Mietvertrag (rental contract).

ex: Ich bin Teil einer tollen Gemeinschaft.
n: Also: municipality (Gemeinde). 'Gemeinschaftsgefühl' = sense of community; 'Europäische Gemeinschaft' = European Community.
'Work-Life-Balance' is a widely used Anglicism. 'Gleichgewicht' is the native German equivalent.

ex: Datenschutz ist in Deutschland sehr wichtig.
n: Germany has extremely strict data-protection laws (DSGVO/GDPR). Germans take privacy very seriously.
Parking fines start at €10; speed-camera fines can be steep. Riding without a ticket: a €60 fine.
Borrowed from English/French. 'Im Budget bleiben' = to stay within budget. 'Budgetplanung' = budget planning.
Germany invented the youth-hostel concept (1914). Jugendherbergen remain affordable and widespread.
'Jemanden begeistern' = to enthuse someone. 'Begeistert sein von' = to be enthusiastic about.
Posted in every German apartment building. Covers noise curfew (Nachtruhe, 10pm–6am) and quiet Sundays.
Since 2024 Germany allows dual citizenship. Requires ~5 years' residence, B1 German, and a citizenship test.
Less widely accepted than in the UK/US. The EC-Karte (Girocard) is more common in Germany.

ex: Die Struktur des Projekts ist klar.
n: 'Strukturieren' = to structure. 'Gut strukturiert' = well-structured — a key concept in academic writing.
'Zusammenfassen' = to summarise. 'Eine Zusammenfassung erstellen' = to write a summary.
'Eine Diagnose stellen' = to make a diagnosis. 'Fehldiagnose' = misdiagnosis.

ex: Ärger vermeiden ist besser als ihn zu lösen.
n: 'Sich ärgern über' = to be annoyed about. 'Ärger machen' = to cause trouble. Very common in spoken German.

ex: Die Kamera hat eine hohe Auflösung.
n: Frontkamera = front camera; Rückkamera = rear camera; Stereokamera = stereo camera (used in ADAS).

ex: Die Katze schläft die ganze Zeit.
n: Most popular pet in Germany. 'Katzenhaar' = cat hair. Renting with a cat: check your contract first.

ex: Am Montag fängt die Woche an.
n: All weekdays are masculine (der Montag … der Sonntag). 'am Montag' = on Monday.

ex: Diese Woche habe ich viel zu tun.
n: 'pro Woche' = per week; 'unter der Woche' = on weekdays; 'das Wochenende' = weekend.

ex: Die Miete zahle ich jeden Monat.
n: 'im Monat' = per month. 'der Monatsanfang' / 'das Monatsende' = start / end of the month.
'am Wochenende' = at the weekend. 'Schönes Wochenende!' = Have a nice weekend!
Tells the hour: 'um 8 Uhr' = at 8 o'clock. 'Wie viel Uhr ist es?' = What time is it?

ex: Das Essen steht auf dem Tisch.
n: 'den Tisch decken' = to set the table; 'der Schreibtisch' = desk.
Plural takes an umlaut: die Stühle. 'der Sessel' = armchair.

ex: Ich gehe um elf ins Bett.
n: 'ins Bett gehen' = to go to bed; 'das Bett machen' = to make the bed.

ex: Das Bad ist am Ende des Flurs.
n: 'das Badezimmer' = bathroom; 'baden' = to bathe/swim; 'ein Bad nehmen' = to take a bath.

ex: Der Weg zum Bahnhof ist kurz.
n: 'auf dem Weg' = on the way; 'der Radweg' = cycle path; 'Viel Erfolg auf deinem Weg!'
'Wie ist das Wetter?' = What's the weather like? 'der Wetterbericht' = weather forecast.

ex: Der Alltag kann manchmal stressig sein.
n: A massive theme in A2 tests. 'Im Alltag' = in everyday life.
Used for professional fields, study areas, or physical zones.
Female: die Bürgerin. Root word for 'Bürgeramt' (registration office).

ex: Wir müssen die Details noch besprechen.
n: Borrowed word, plural usually takes an 's'. 'Ins Detail gehen' = to go into detail.

ex: Er hat einen guten Eindruck gemacht.
n: 'Einen Eindruck hinterlassen' = to leave an impression. 'Der erste Eindruck' = first impression.

ex: Das Wetter hat großen Einfluss auf meine Laune.
n: 'Einfluss haben auf' = to have an influence on.

ex: Das Ergebnis des Tests ist positiv.
n: Key testing and business vocabulary. 'Zu einem Ergebnis kommen' = to come to a conclusion.

ex: Die Reise war ein tolles Erlebnis.
n: Different from 'Erfahrung' (accumulated knowledge). Erlebnis is a specific, memorable event.

ex: Das ist genau das Gegenteil von dem, was ich will.
n: 'Im Gegenteil' = on the contrary.

ex: In den Bergen gibt es viele Gefahren.
n: Adjective: gefährlich (dangerous). 'In Gefahr sein' = to be in danger.

ex: Das ist eine gute Gelegenheit, Deutsch zu üben.
n: More specific than 'Möglichkeit' (possibility). It means a favorable time/chance to do something.
'Gewalt anwenden' = to use force/violence.
A very culturally loaded German word denoting where one feels they truly belong.
'Hilfe rufen' = to call for help. 'Erste Hilfe' = first aid.

ex: Der Inhalt des Buches ist spannend.
n: 'Inhaltsverzeichnis' = table of contents.
'Einen Kompromiss schließen' = to make a compromise.
Used in school (academic performance), sports, and work (achievements).
Also: 'Briefmarke' (postage stamp).

ex: Eine große Menge Menschen war da.
n: 'Jede Menge' = a whole lot / loads of.

ex: Er ist Mitglied in einem Verein.
n: Compound of Mit- (with) + Glied (link/limb). Essential for German club (Verein) culture.

ex: Das Opfer wurde ins Krankenhaus gebracht.
n: Used heavily in news reports regarding accidents or crimes.
Opposite of 'das Glück'. 'Pech haben' = to be unlucky.
Collective noun for spectators at a concert, play, or presentation.

ex: Der Raum ist sehr groß und hell.
n: Often synonymous with 'Zimmer', but broader (can mean physical space or a large hall).

ex: Jeder hat das Recht auf freie Meinung.
n: 'Recht haben' = to be right. 'Jura / Rechte studieren' = to study law.

ex: Nimm bitte Rücksicht auf die Nachbarn.
n: 'Rücksicht nehmen auf' (+ Acc) = to be considerate of.
'Umweltschutz' = environmental protection; 'Datenschutz' = data protection.
'Tatsächlich' (adverb) = actually / in fact.

ex: Die Ursache des Problems ist unklar.
n: 'Keine Ursache!' = Don't mention it! (Polite reply to 'Thank you').
From the reflexive verb 'sich verhalten' (to behave).
